channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world

item.title: EU vows tough countermeasures to US tariffs
item.description: European Union chief Ursula von der Leyen vowed that U.S. tariffs on steel and aluminum “will not go unanswered” and will trigger tough countermeasures from the 27-nation bloc.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Venezuelan planes sent to US for deportation flights return to country with nearly 200 deportees
item.description: Two planes sent by Venezuela returned to the country from El Paso, Texas, on Monday with nearly 200 Venezuelans who were in the U.S. illegally.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Haitian migrants share harrowing stories of abuse as Dominican Republic ramps up deportations
item.description: Dominican officials have been deporting at least 10,000 Haitian immigrants a week under a harsh new policy widely criticized by civil organizations.
item.dc:creator: Dánica Coto, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Trump will host Jordan's King Abdullah II as he escalates pressure on his Gaza resettlement plan
item.description: President Donald Trump will host Jordan’s King Abdullah II at the White House as he escalates pressure on the Arab nation to take in refugees from Gaza as part of his audacious plan to remake the Middle East.
item.dc:creator: Zeke Miller, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Trump's taunting of Canada will pay dividends: Bill O'Reilly
item.description: “Those of use who live in the real world — not the ideological, crazy world — we know it’s just hyperbole.
item.dc:creator: NewsNation

item.title: UN suspends its humanitarian work in Houthi rebels' stronghold in Yemen after more staff detentions
item.description: CAIRO (AP) — The United Nations said Monday it suspended its humanitarian operations in the stronghold of Yemen’s Houthi rebels after they detained eight more U.N. staffers, affecting the global response to one of the world’s worst humanitarian disasters. In a statement, the U.N.
item.dc:creator: SAMY MAGDY, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: What Mexico's richest man thinks Trump should do in his second term
item.description: Mexican businessman Carlos Slim, one of the world’s richest people, holds forth once a year on a wide range of topics.
item.dc:creator: María Verza, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: At least 55 are dead after a bus plunged into gorge in Guatemala
item.description: GUATEMALA CITY (AP) — At least 55 people are dead after their bus plunged into a gorge and landed under a bridge Monday on the outskirts of Guatemala’s capital.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Trump says he may withhold aid to Jordan, Egypt if they don’t take in Palestinians
item.description: President Trump said Monday he would consider withholding aid to Jordan and Egypt if they do not agree to take in Palestinians as part of a plan for the U.S. to take over and develop the Gaza Strip.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, The Hill

item.title: Former Colombian President Uribe denies bribery and witness tampering charges in historic trial
item.description: BOGOTA, Colombia (AP) — Former Colombian President Álvaro Uribe on Monday denied charges of bribery and witness tampering as he made his opening statement in the first criminal trial of a former president in the country’s history.
item.dc:creator: ASTRID SUAREZ and MANUEL RUEDA, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Trump says he has talked to Xi since inauguration
item.description: President Trump said he has spoken to Chinese President Xi Jinping since his swearing in last month, suggesting they have spoken in the days since he imposed tariffs on Beijing. “I happen to like him a lot, President Xi,” Trump said.
item.dc:creator: Alex Gangitano, The Hill

item.title: Trump signs off on new steel and aluminum tariffs
item.description: President Trump on Monday signed proclamations to bolster tariffs on all steel and aluminum imports into the United States and crack down on attempts by China and Russia to evade penalties. Trump in 2018 announced 25 percent tariffs on steel and 10 percent tariffs on aluminum imports.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, The Hill

item.title: US, Russia relations nearing collapse: Kremlin
item.description: Russian Deputy Foreign Minister Sergei Ryabkov said Monday that the U.S.-Russia relations are on the verge of a major breakdown. Despite President Trump claiming that he spoke with Russian President Vladimir Putin over the weekend, the Kremlin has refused to confirm the call.
item.dc:creator: Ailia Zehra, The Hill

item.title: Push to eliminate renewable energy goals in Puerto Rico sparks outrage as outages persist
item.description: SAN JUAN, Puerto Rico (AP) — Puerto Rico legislators on Monday held a contentious public hearing on a governor-backed bill that would eliminate renewable energy goals and extend operations of the island's lone coal-fired plant, long accused of polluting low-income communities.
item.dc:creator: DÁNICA COTO, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Ecuador's presidential election goes to runoff between conservative incumbent, leftist lawyer
item.description: Ecuador will choose its next president in a runoff election in April between conservative incumbent Daniel Noboa and leftist lawyer Luisa González. Crime is a major issue for voters.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump steps up his 2018 tariffs on steel and aluminum, risking inflation on promise of more jobs
item.description: President Donald Trump on Monday removed the exceptions and exemptions from his 2018 tariffs on steel, meaning that all steel imports will be taxed at a minimum of 25%.
item.dc:creator: Josh Boak, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Video: Rare ‘Black Demon’ fish spotted alive for first time
item.description: The scary-looking fish is usually found in water depths of 1,500 meters, where little to no sunlight reaches.
item.dc:creator: Celeste Houmard, NewsNation

item.title: Hamas releases 3 frail-looking Israeli hostages for Palestinian prisoners under Gaza ceasefire
item.description: DEIR AL-BALAH, Gaza Strip (AP) — Hamas-led militants released three gaunt, frail-looking Israeli hostages and Israel freed nearly 200 Palestinian prisoners Saturday in the latest exchange of a ceasefire that has paused 16 months of war in Gaza.
item.dc:creator: WAFAA SHURAFA, MOHAMMAD JAHJOUH and TIA GOLDENBERG, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Mother, son die from food poisoning at Dominican Republic resort
item.description: A grieving family filed a lawsuit after a mother and son died from food poisoning while vacationing at a Dominican Republic resort.
item.dc:creator: Jordan Unger, NewsNation

item.title: Leaders from eastern and southern Africa call for a ceasefire and negotiations in Congo
item.description: KAMPALA, Uganda (AP) — Leaders from eastern and southern Africa on Saturday called for an immediate ceasefire in eastern Congo, where rebels are threatening to overthrow the Congolese government, but also urged Congo's president to directly negotiate with them.
item.dc:creator: RODNEY MUHUMUZA, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Former Hamas hostage released 491 days after he was kidnapped and his family was slaughtered
item.description: Eli Sharabi, who was released after spending 491 days in Hamas captivity, will return home without his wife or two daughters, who were killed on Oct. 7, 2023.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: New report by panel of experts claims killer nurse Lucy Letby is innocent
item.description: Convicted of killing seven babies and attempting to kill seven more, Lucy Letby's conviction is being questioned by a new report that claims she is a victim of a miscarriage of justice.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: World's largest religious gathering in India underway as hundreds of millions visit 45-day festival
item.description: The largest religious gathering in the world is taking place in India from Jan. 13 to Feb. 26. It is a significant test for the country to showcase Hinduism and culture.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Brazil's socialist president tells citizens not to buy expensive grocery items as food prices rise
item.description: Brazilian President Luiz Inácio Lula da Silva said in a video message posted to social media that citizens should not buy expensive food items to deal with rising food prices.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: A look at the hostages taken to Gaza by Hamas, by the numbers
item.description: Hamas says it will delay the next release of hostages in the Gaza Strip after accusing Israel of violating their fragile ceasefire that began last month but appears increasingly at risk of collapse.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: One of the victims of Sweden's deadliest mass shooting called his fiancée to say he loved her
item.description: OREBRO, Sweden (AP) — Wounded and bleeding, Salim Karim Iskef managed to video-call his fiancée to tell her he loved her one last time before he died this week in what was Sweden's deadliest mass shooting, The 28-year-old asked Kareen Alia to look after his mother and herself before the call ended.
item.dc:creator: ALEKSANDAR FURTULA, SERGEI GRITS and STEFANIE DAZIO, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: AI, scientists unite to decipher old scrolls charred by Vesuvius volcano
item.description: Hundreds of papyrus scrolls were found in the 1750s in the remains of a lavish villa at the Roman town of Herculaneum.
item.dc:creator: JILL LAWLESS and PAN PYLAS, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: 5 takeaways as Trump’s Gaza Strip proposal reverberates
item.description: Aftershocks reverberated Wednesday from President Trump’s declaration the previous day that the United States should take “long-term ownership” of Gaza and that the Palestinians who live there should be moved.
item.dc:creator: Niall Stanage, WGN 9

item.title: Arab nations decry Trump proposal for US to take over Gaza
item.description: Palestinian President Mahmoud Abbas called for the United Nations to “protect the Palestinian people.
item.dc:creator: Robert Sherman, NewsNation

item.title: Jeffries amps up call for two-state solution after Trump push to seize Gaza
item.description: House Minority Leader Hakeem Jeffries (D-N.Y.) on Wednesday flatly rejected President Trump’s proposal for the United States to take over the Gaza Strip, suggesting it would undermine ongoing efforts to free Israeli hostages and bring peace to the volatile region.
item.dc:creator: Mike Lillis, WGN 9

item.title: US allies, adversaries reject Trump suggestion to 'take over' Gaza Strip
item.description: When the president was asked about possibly sending in U.S. troops to Gaza, he said America will “do what is necessary.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, NewsNation

item.title: U.S. allies, adversaries reject Trump suggestion to 'take over' Gaza Strip
item.description: Both U.S. allies and adversaries alike rejected President Donald Trump’s suggestion that the U.S. “take over” the war-torn Gaza Strip and turn it into the “Riviera of the Middle East.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, WGN 9

item.title: Saudi Arabia says it won't establish ties with Israel without creation of Palestinian state
item.description: Saudi Arabia said on Wednesday it would not establish ties with Israel without the creation of a Palestinian state.
item.dc:creator: Reuters, NewsNation

item.title: Trump floats US taking over Gaza Strip
item.description: President Trump on Tuesday floated the idea of the U.S. taking over the Gaza Strip, saying America should be responsible for clearing the territory of rubble and unexploded bombs and proposing an “economic development.
item.dc:creator: Laura Kelly, WGN 9
